exemplar.title:
To what extent does pre-treating bacterial extract of Staphylococcus
Epidermidis with bromelain from pineapple influence the effectiveness of
Tetracycline?

To what extent does pre-treating bacterial extract of Staphylococcus
Epidermidis with bromelain from pineapple influence the effectiveness of

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Accurate and effective communication of the global problem, enzyme mechanism and bacterium link
Clearly stated, narrowly focused and testable research question
Comprehensive explanations that balance depth and clarity for a non-specialist reader
weaknesses: Bromelain concentration is unquantified and some safety/sterility steps are glossed over
Controls (positive vs negative) could be more explicitly distinguished
Minor labeling error in the variables table detracts slightly from clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently meets the top band descriptors: the topic is communicated accurately and effectively, the research question is focused and testable, and the methodology is largely appropriate and well justified. Minor lapses in quantifying bromelain concentration and fully detailing safety controls prevent a flawless 6 in every strand, but overall performance remains at the top band.

strengths: Coherent and relevant application of primary research articles, WHO data and textbook sources
Accurate and consistent use of advanced scientific terminology throughout
Strong integration of biofilm mechanisms and efflux-pump gene discussion
weaknesses: Occasional overgeneralisation of bromelainâs specific molecular targets without direct evidence
Some citation details (currency context, source precision) require refinement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear and effective use of source material and consistently accurate terminology, fitting the top band. However, occasional over-generalisation about specific mechanistic targets and minor citation imprecision prevent the very highest mark of 6 in this criterion.

strengths: Well-justified selection and application of non-parametric statistical tests with clear linkage to the research question
Logical, coherent argument flow from hypothesis through results to evaluation
Thoughtful evaluation of methodological limitations (enzyme purity, temperature) with actionable suggestions
weaknesses: Depth of evaluation is uneven; some confounding variables (disc diffusion method, pineapple extract composition) are underexplored
Mechanistic conclusions occasionally veer into speculation without direct experimental support
Critical discussion could more consistently reference preliminary assay justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis and argumentation are strong, with robust statistical reasoning and clear relevance to the research question, placing the work in the 7â9 band. The evaluation is generally well supported but uneven in depth and sometimes overly speculative. These factors warrant a score of 7 within the top band.

strengths: Adheres to EE structure with title page, headings, figures and tables logically placed
Layout conventions (in-text citations, bibliography, word count) are generally correct
Word count is stated and within the prescribed limit
weaknesses: Inconsistent page numbering and indentation of contents entries
Varied figure/table caption formatting and missing source credits
Minor formatting issues in tables (split tables, misaligned captions)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation follows the prescribed format and includes the necessary layout conventions, meriting the top band. However, inconsistencies in page numbering, caption style and minor formatting lapses prevent a flawless execution, so a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The essay follows the prescribed EE format with title page, abstract substitute (introduction), contents page, clearly labelled sections and sub-headings.  Tables, figures and appendices are logically placed and referenced.

No major structural elements are missing, and the sequencing supports comprehension; hence it fits the top presentation band (3-4) with maximum 4 marks.

Layout conventions (table of contents, headings hierarchy, figure captions, in-text citations, bibliography) are present and generally correct.  Word count is stated and appears within limit, though page numbers are inconsistently displayed in the extract.

Minor formatting issues (some tables split, occasional misaligned captions) prevent perfect execution, situating the work solidly in the 3-4 band at 3 marks.

The argument flows logically: background â hypothesis â method â results â statistical testing â interpretation â evaluation â conclusion.  Each section builds coherently, and signposting keeps the reader oriented.

Minor lapses (repetition in background; some long procedural lists) introduce slight inconsistency, yet overall structure of reasoning remains clear, cohesive and persuasive, meriting the higher end of the 7-9 band (score 8).

The student introduces the global problem of antibiotic resistance, explains bromelainâs biochemical action, and links these ideas directly to the chosen bacterium and antibiotic.  Background, hypothesis and significance are clearly framed, showing an integrated understanding of the topic.

â¢  Definitions (e.g., intrinsic vs acquired resistance) are precise and consistently accurate.
â¢  Explanatory passages are comprehensive yet focused, allowing a non-specialist reader to follow the rationale without extraneous digressions.

Overall, topic communication is accurate, complete and effective, meeting the descriptors for the top band.

Source material spans primary research articles, WHO data and textbook explanations.  Citations are consistently embedded to support claims about efflux pumps, bromelain activity and biofilm formation.

The student moves beyond descriptionâe.g., linking efflux-pump genes to observed inhibition zonesâshowing coherent application.  Occasional over-generalisation (e.g., bromelain directly targeting TolC in S. epidermidis without specific evidence) prevents the highest excellence, but relevance and integration remain strong, fitting 5 within the top band.

Analysis: Data are processed correctly (means, SD, Kruskal-Wallis, Dunnâs post-hoc) and interpreted in relation to the RQ.  The choice of non-parametric tests is justified through distribution checks, demonstrating methodological thought.

Relevance: Discussion consistently links statistical outcomes to efflux-pump theory and enzyme activity, keeping focus on the research question.

Limitations are acknowledged (enzyme purity, sub-optimal temperature), but biochemical mechanisms are sometimes asserted without direct evidence, keeping the work at a strong but not perfect level within the 7-9 band (score 8).

The student details variables, control groups, materials, preliminary enzyme assays, replicate number (n = 30) and non-parametric statistics.  Controls for temperature, pH, disc size and incubation are justified, showing clear selection rationale.

Minor weaknesses: bromelain concentration is unquantified and some safety/sterility steps are briefly glossed over.  Despite this, the overall range and appropriateness of methods is strong and clearly argued, meriting the upper band though not flawless, hence a solid 5.

The student evaluates significance of statistical findings and considers sources of error (enzyme purity, incubation temperature).  Alternative methods (ultrafiltration) are suggested, showing reflective thinking.

However, depth of critique is uneven; some confounding variables (exact pineapple composition, disc diffusion limitations) are only briefly mentioned.  Conclusions are generally well-supported but lean on speculative mechanistic explanations.  This aligns with a good, well-supported evaluation but short of âexcellentâ, warranting 7 in the 7-9 band.

The research question is explicitly stated in the title and repeated in the introduction.  It is narrow (one bacterium, one enzyme extract, one antibiotic) and testable through a single measurable dependent variable (zone of inhibition).  The wording (âto what extentâ¦â) invites quantitative evaluation that the subsequent method and statistics address.

The studyâs scope is appropriate for an EE and stays tightly aligned to the RQ throughout, fulfilling all descriptors of a focused and appropriate question in the 5-6 band.

Scientific terms (endoprotease, proton-motive force, Kruskal-Wallis ANOVA) are used accurately and consistently.  Graphs, tables and statistical language follow biological conventions.  Misuse of terminology is rare and does not impede clarity.

Consequently, the work meets the descriptor for accurate and consistent terminology at the top of the band (6).

annotations.comment: The introduction effectively outlines the global burden of resistance but does not explicitly link to the research question. Add a sentence connecting significance to bromelain pretreatment.
Dependent variable measurement method using a digital caliper is precise, demonstrating methodological rigour.
The title is very long and split awkwardly across lines. Consider refining it for conciseness and clarity to meet formatting conventions.
The word count is correctly stated and within the limit, satisfying the formatting requirement.
Indentation of sub-headings in the contents page is inconsistent. Align subsection entries uniformly to improve navigation.
Several entries in the contents lack page numbers, reducing clarity. Add missing page references for complete presentation.
Footnote citation style varies between numeric and parenthetical. Use a uniform citation format throughout to improve consistency.
The mechanism of bromelain as an endopeptidase is accurately described, demonstrating strong subject knowledge.
The claim that bromelain is the âmain inhibitorâ of growth overgeneralizes without specific data. Request clarification or citation of experimental evidence.
Detail on biofilm formation and adhesins (Aap, SasG) effectively contextualises S. epidermidis; strong integration of source material.
The citation for âover $2 billionâ lacks currency context and precise source details. Specify units (USD) and full reference.
The explanation of tetracycline resistance via Tet(K)/Tet(L) and HGT is accurate and demonstrates effective use of terminology.
The paragraph repeats the phrase âintrinsic and acquired resistance.â Revise to improve readability and avoid redundancy.
Figure 2.1 caption is concise but lacks a source statement. Add âAdapted fromâ¦â or similar to credit origin.
The principal experiment description clearly defines controls but could distinguish positive vs. negative controls more explicitly.
Variables table mistakenly labels two rows as âIndependent Variable.â Correct the second label to âTreatment Disc Typeâ or merge rows.
The inline formatting for degrees (e.g., â60C^{\circ}â) is incorrect. Use $$60^\\circ\mathrm{C}$$ for standard scientific notation.
Equation for KruskalâWallis statistic is correctly formatted and demonstrates strong statistical understanding.
Graph captions vary in style (e.g., âGraph 4.1â vs. âGraph 4.2:â). Standardise numbering and phrasing for consistency.
Table 4.2 is titled âANOVA test resultsâ but presents non-parametric H statistic. Relabel to âKruskalâWallis ANOVA resultsâ to reflect method.
Dunnâs post-hoc test results are clearly tabulated with p-values and significance; excellent presentation of follow-up analysis.
Controlled variables table is thorough; the rationale for temperature control shows clear understanding of experimental design.
The limitation of 25 Â°C for bromelain activity is noted. Suggest referencing preliminary assay results here to justify enzymatic functionality at this temperature.
Preliminary testing section succinctly describes protease assay and contamination check; methodology is well justified.
Rationale for selecting a non-parametric test is well argued, showing clear critical thinking regarding data distribution.
Discussion correctly links statistical outcomes to efflux-pump inhibition but asserts mechanistic details without direct evidence. Qualify these as proposed mechanisms.
Evaluation acknowledges temperature and purity limitations and proposes ultrafiltration; actionable reflection that strengthens methodological critique.
Conclusion introduces Salvia fruticosa synergy, a new concept not covered in background. Either integrate earlier or focus solely on bromelain.
